patch_location.anchor: Specific, quantitative, station-named, timestamped before the data — with the prediction document anchored independently from any observation file.
patch_location.anchor_uniqueness_verified: grep -c against generate-html.js returns exactly 1 occurrence of the full string "with the prediction document anchored independently from any observation file". patch_location.replacement: Specific, quantitative, station-named, timestamped before the data — with the prediction document independently timestamped separately from any observation data.

rationale: ISS-1589 reports that the Overview-tab falsifiability-module Condition 2 description uses the phrase "anchored independently from any observation file", but the verbatim source phrase in the Evaluation-Guide eg-falsifiability section (generate-html.js line 1748) reads "independently timestamped separately from any observation data". The module is a distillation surface — its Condition 2 row was intended to be a faithful compression of the eg-falsifiability Condition 2 paragraph, not an analyst paraphrase. The curmudgeon EXP-256 proposal c1 H5 noted: "Anchored is technically more precise (matches OTS terminology used in the Timestamp Error tab), but it introduces jargon a casual reader may not parse without expanding the section." Decider triaged this as needing a generate-html.js edit. The patch swaps "anchored independently from any observation file" for "independently timestamped separately from any observation data" — a verbatim copy from the source line. This costs a small amount of OTS-specific precision (anchored → timestamped is generic-vs-cryptographic) but gains: (a) verbatim distillation faithfulness; (b) accessibility for casual readers; (c) cross-surface consistency (Overview module and Eval-Guide source now use identical wording for Condition 2). Readers who want OTS precision follow the fm-jump link to eg-falsifiability and from there the Timestamp Error analysis link.
kernel_of_truth: N/A — this is an internal-consistency fix between two project surfaces, not a dome-attack expansion. The kernel concept does not apply (no dome claim is being engaged here). The verbatim-source principle is the directive: when a UI module compresses prose from a long-form source, the module wording should come from the source, not from analyst elaboration around it.
holes_addressed: EXP-256 proposal c1 H5 (minor): module Condition 2 uses "anchored independently from any observation file" but the eg-falsifiability source uses "independently timestamped separately from any observation data". Resolved by patching the module to match source verbatim for the anchoring clause.
holes_NOT_addressed: EXP-256 proposal c1 H1 (moderate, WCAG contrast) — separate ISS scope, not covered here
EXP-256 proposal c1 H2 (moderate, asymmetric 0/69 vs pending framing) — separate scope
EXP-256 proposal c1 H3 (moderate, ds- prefix convention) — separate scope
EXP-256 proposal c1 H4 (minor, stale anchor line numbers in proposal) — meta, no on-page impact
EXP-256 proposal c1 H6 (minor, 0/69 denominator for condition 3) — separate scope, curmudgeon preference is to keep visual rhythm

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon explicitly said "Acceptable as-is" and suggested the swap is only worthwhile "if a future revision wants to soften". Why are we patching a minor hole the curmudgeon was prepared to accept?
anticipated_objections.response: Three reasons. (a) Decider triaged this as an actionable ISS (severity=minor, age>7d, default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST) and explicitly noted "Requires generate-html.js edit — yeet to analyst" — i.e., the decider accepted the curmudgeon recommendation that a swap is acceptable and chose to apply it. (b) The CLAUDE.md module-design principle for surface distillations is "verbatim from source, not embellished" — "acceptable as-is" was the curmudgeon's posture given there were higher-priority holes (WCAG, asymmetric status), but the substantive critique stands. (c) The cost of the patch is one phrase swap (zero on-page regression risk, no truth-content shift); the benefit is small but real. Closing-no-patch would be the alternative and is defensible, but actively making the cross-surface consistency match is the more thorough fix.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Anchored" is genuinely more precise than "timestamped" — OTS specifically anchors a hash to the Bitcoin blockchain; "timestamped" is the generic term. Are we trading away precision the technical reader values?
anticipated_objections.response: Slightly, yes, but the trade is favorable. (a) The Overview module is a brief-reminder surface aimed at casual readers; precision for the technical reader lives in the Timestamp Error tab (Part 6) which the fm-jump link plus the source's own embedded link both lead to. Line 1915 in Part 6 still uses "anchored independently after August 12" in its technical-prose context — the OTS-specific term is preserved where it serves the careful reader. (b) The module already does not attempt full OTS rigor: it does not name "OpenTimestamps", does not mention the SHA-256 hash, does not mention calendar servers or Bitcoin attestations. It is already compressed; "timestamped" is consistent with that compression level. (c) The source phrase "independently timestamped separately from any observation data" already names the operational fact being asserted (two separate timestamps for two separate documents); the "anchored" terminology is more about HOW the timestamping is implemented (OTS), not WHAT property is required.

anticipated_objections.objection: The replacement_text still uses "timestamped before the data" at the front — also an analyst phrasing not verbatim from source. Why not fix that too?
anticipated_objections.response: Out of scope for this ISS. ISS-1589 specifically identifies "anchored independently from any observation file" as the offending phrase; the curmudgeon c1 H5 hole is narrowly scoped to the anchoring clause. "Timestamped before the data" is a separate compression-clause that has not been flagged as embellishment by curmudgeon. Expanding the patch scope unilaterally would be analyst overreach. If a future curmudgeon cycle flags it, address then.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should the source-side prose (line 1748) be changed instead to use "anchored", upgrading the source to the module's more-precise language?
anticipated_objections.response: No — module is downstream of source. The module exists to distill the source; if there is a mismatch between source and distillation, the distillation moves to match the source, not vice versa. The source's "independently timestamped" phrasing has been stable since EXP-256 c1 review and has not been challenged on its own merits. Changing the source to match the analyst-elaborated module reverses the proper direction of fix.

test_impact: None expected. This is a string-content change inside an HTML template literal in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Does not touch DATA blobs, chart arrays, counts, or any computed value. tests/run.sh checks should be unaffected (verification: the patched string is rendered into docs/index.html on build; decider should rebuild via node build.js html and confirm the rendered string in docs/index.html matches "with the prediction document independently timestamped separately from any observation data" and that there are no other regressions).
review_class_override_note: Tracker entry declares review_class=deep-attack (set by analyst-baby on orphan-pickup escalation). Authoring analyst declares review_class=verification per PROP-025 author-honesty guidance: this EXP is a single-phrase wordsmithing patch matching source verbatim — no new argument, no defender-pivot, no verdict shift. Curmudgeon re-review should be batchable with other verification items per Step 8a, not consume a full singleton run. The deep-attack escalation was overcautious; the actual work is closing-out a curmudgeon recommendation that was already explicitly suggested by the curmudgeon prose.
summary_for_decider: EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1589 patches the Overview-tab falsifiability-module Condition 2 description in build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1522. Single-phrase swap: "anchored independently from any observation file" → "independently timestamped separately from any observation data" (verbatim match to source line 1748). Resolves curmudgeon EXP-256 proposal c1 H5 (minor) and decider ISS-1589. NO truth-content change, NO data change, build rebuild required (node build.js html). review_class=verification (downgraded from baby-escalation deep-attack — see review_class_override_note). Recommend close ISS-1589 with fix_details linking to this EXP after integration + rebuild verification.
